title: B002: Implement Kubernetes-interacting packages
summary: Review of fixes addressing three bugs flagged by greptile-apps[bot]: (1) EnsureDatabaseUser now short-circuits Grant creation when User is not ready, (2) RunJob normalizes API-server container defaults before spec comparison to prevent false-positive Job re-creation, (3) LoadPolicyFromConfigMap returns an explicit error when policy.yaml parses to nil. All three fixes are correct, well-tested, and consistent with existing patterns.

tests_checklist.item: Edge cases covered: empty/null YAML, user-not-ready, API-server defaults
New test TestEnsureDatabaseUser_updatesGrant_whenUserReady covers Grant update when User is ready
New tests TestLoadPolicyFromConfigMap_emptyValue and _nullYAML cover nil-parse edge cases
Existing tests updated to reflect new behavior (idempotent, complete scenarios)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (gomega assertions, error wrapping, controllerutil usage)
Functions are small and single-responsibility (normalizePodSpecDefaults, normalizeContainerDefaults)
No dead code or commented-out code
CC-0005 feature tags present in all comments
DeepCopy used before mutation in RunJob to avoid mutating caller's spec

security_checklist.item: No user input handled directly (all inputs are K8s API objects)
Error messages do not leak sensitive data

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible for each fix
normalizePodSpecDefaults is a private helper scoped to job package

dry_yagni_checklist.item: normalizeContainerDefaults shared between Containers and InitContainers
testJobWithDefaults helper avoids repeating defaults in multiple tests

fail_fast_checklist.item: EnsureDatabaseUser returns early when User is not ready
LoadPolicyFromConfigMap returns error for nil parse result instead of (nil, nil)

defensive_checklist.item: RunJob DeepCopies desired spec before normalization to avoid caller mutation
LoadPolicyFromConfigMap validates parse result is non-nil

suggested_improvements: Minor: normalizePodSpecDefaults unconditionally sets ImagePullPolicy to PullIfNotPresent, but the API server defaults to PullAlways for :latest tags. If a caller constructs a Job with image 'foo:latest' and zero-value ImagePullPolicy, the stored spec (PullAlways) would differ from the normalized desired spec (PullIfNotPresent), causing a false-positive Job re-creation. In practice this is unlikely since production operators use pinned image tags, but consider adding a tag-aware defaulting check or documenting this limitation (job.go:146).
